title: The Storm Pushes Them Forward
description: On pages 18–21, the storm pushes them forward focuses on Luffy, Zoro, Nami, Usopp as a storm, Smoker, and a mysterious intervention push the Straw Hats out of Loguetown and toward the Grand Line.
scene: On pages 18–21, the storm pushes them forward focuses on Luffy, Zoro, Nami, Usopp as a storm, Smoker, and a mysterious intervention push the Straw Hats out of Loguetown and toward the Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: I can't hand over this sword to anyone...

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: I'll be going on ahead then.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Why didn't you cut me down!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You're so lucky to be a boy, Zoro...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You dare go easy on me in a serious duel just because of the fact that women aren't as physically powerful as men!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Of course, I shouldn't expect someone like you to ever understand what wishing to be born a man would feel like!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: But know that I didn't pick up this sword for fun!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: It's your damn exis- fence that bothers me, not your gender!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: Your face is exactly like my friend who died long ago!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: And now you go on spoutin' the same things she did!

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: So stop copying her, you rip-off!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What? I've never heard anything so childish in my life before!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I've always lived being me and only me my entire life!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I have no idea who this friend of yours even is! In fact, I should be the one who's complaining here!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: Maybe she's the one who copied me!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm a captain of the Marine Head- Quarters.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And I won't allow you to set sail from here!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: I don't have time for runts.

summary: This part closes East Blue with a storm-driven escape and the crew’s first real step toward the Grand Line.
